subject: ap-government
title: Argument Essay — Balance of Power Between the President and Congress
stimulus: The power of the executive branch in relation to the legislative branch has varied over time. Develop an argument that takes a position on the appropriate balance of power between the president and Congress.

parts.prompt: Respond to the prompt with a defensible claim or thesis that establishes a line of reasoning. (Use at least one piece of evidence from one of the following foundational documents: Declaration of Independence, The Federalist 51, The Federalist 70.)
parts.scoring_notes: Thesis must take a clear position and establish a line of reasoning. Does NOT need foundational documents.

scoring_points.description: Row A — Thesis/Claim: Respond to the prompt with a defensible claim or thesis that establishes a line of reasoning.

required_elements: Defensible claim establishing a line of reasoning
alternatives.correct_example: “The power of the executive should be strengthened relative to Congress because Congress is slow to react and sometimes ends up in gridlock.”

required_elements: Defensible claim establishing a line of reasoning
alternatives.correct_example: “The power of Congress should be strengthened relative to the president because Congress is the representative of the will of the people.”

required_elements: Defensible claim establishing a line of reasoning
alternatives.correct_example: “The power of the executive and legislative branches should be equal because no one branch should become too powerful/limited government.”

wrong_examples: “The power of the executive and legislative branches of government are important because there is a balance of power.” Do not respond to the prompt
“The executive branch is the most effective branch of government because the president has many constitutional powers.”
common_traps: Do NOT require foundational documents in thesis
A because clause with reasons IS a line of reasoning

parts.prompt: Support your claim with at least TWO pieces of specific and relevant evidence. One piece of evidence must come from one of the foundational documents listed. A second piece of evidence can come from any other foundational document or from course concepts.
parts.scoring_notes: TIERED SCORING: Award HIGHEST tier met. Each tier 1pt progressive. Tier 2+ requires Row A.

scoring_points.description: Evidence Tier 1: One relevant piece of evidence.
Evidence Tier 2: One specific piece supporting thesis OR two relevant. Requires Row A.

required_elements: One specific piece supporting thesis OR two relevant
alternatives.correct_example: Uses evidence to directly support thesis.

scoring_points.description: Evidence Tier 3: Two specific pieces supporting thesis, one from listed foundational document. Requires Row A.

alternatives.correct_example: Uses foundational doc + concept supporting thesis.

parts.prompt: Use reasoning to explain why your evidence supports your claim or thesis.
parts.scoring_notes: Must have earned Row A and at least 2 points in Row B. Listing evidence without explaining how it connects to the thesis does not earn this point.

scoring_points.description: Row C — Reasoning: Use reasoning (classification, process, causation, or comparison) to explain how or why the evidence supports the claim or thesis.

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “The Declaration of Independence expressed concern that the king had too much power, and therefore a stronger legislative branch accountable to the people would be needed to ensure a constitutional government that respects civil liberty.”

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “The Federalist 51 argued that there should be a balance of power between the legislative and executive branches so that one branch does not become overly powerful. This relatively equal balance of power between the branches should be maintained.”

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “The Federalist 70 argued that a strong executive is needed to act to protect the nation which sometimes means the executive branch needs to be the most powerful branch of government.”

required_elements: Reasoning that connects evidence to claim
alternatives.correct_example: “The power of the legislative branch to confirm presidential appointments provides a balance of power between the legislative branch and the executive because it prevents the president from unilaterally staffing the Cabinet who could implement laws that subvert the will of Congress.”

common_traps: Must have earned Row A and at least 2 points in Row B
Simply restating evidence without reasoning does not earn this point

parts.prompt: Respond to an opposing or alternate perspective using refutation, concession, or rebuttal.
parts.scoring_notes: Must have earned Row A. Must describe an alternate perspective AND refute, concede, or rebut it — not merely acknowledge it exists.

scoring_points.description: Row D — Responds to Alternate Perspectives: Respond to an opposing or alternate perspective using rebuttal or refutation.

required_elements: Describes alternate perspective AND rebuts/refutes it
alternatives.correct_example: “While some say the president should be more powerful than Congress because of national security concerns which require immediate action, a more powerful legislature would ensure that those responses are in line with the will of the people.”

required_elements: Describes alternate perspective AND rebuts/refutes it
alternatives.correct_example: “While some say that the legislative branch should be more powerful than the executive branch because the legislature is more closely tied to the will of the people, gridlock in the legislative branch could make fast action more difficult.”

required_elements: Describes alternate perspective AND rebuts/refutes it
alternatives.correct_example: “While some say that the power between the executive and legislative branches should be equal, it created an inefficient, slow system in response to problems.”

wrong_examples: “Many believe the president should be more powerful than Congress because the framers gave the president the most powers in the Constitution.” Describe an alternate perspective but do not refute, concede, or rebut that perspective
“Some would argue that the legislative branch should have more power than the executive branch because it is closer to the voice of the people.”
common_traps: Simply identifying an alternate perspective without rebutting/refuting it does not earn this point
